question: What is the recorded population of the settlement of Zelphar?

documents.title: Zelphar population
documents.text: The Zelphar census records the population as 4,210 residents.

documents.title: Zelphar history
documents.text: Zelphar was founded by river traders many decades ago.

documents.title: Zelphar geography
documents.text: Zelphar lies in the Maren valley with mild winters.

documents.title: Zelphar economy
documents.text: Zelphar's economy centres on weaving and a produce market.

documents.title: Zelphar festivals
documents.text: Zelphar holds a lantern festival each autumn.

documents.title: Zelphar cuisine
documents.text: Zelphar is known locally for a spiced river-fish stew.

documents.title: Zelphar architecture
documents.text: Zelphar's older quarter has timber-framed houses.

documents.title: Zelphar climate
documents.text: Zelphar sees light rainfall spread evenly through the year.

question: In what year was the Kthandri Bridge completed?

documents.title: Kthandri Bridge completion
documents.text: The Kthandri Bridge was completed in the year 1873.

documents.title: Kthandri Bridge design
documents.text: The Kthandri Bridge uses an unusual twin-arch design.

documents.title: Kthandri Bridge repairs
documents.text: The Kthandri Bridge has been repaired after floods.

documents.title: Kthandri Bridge today
documents.text: The Kthandri Bridge now carries a narrow tram line.

documents.title: Kthandri Bridge stone
documents.text: The Kthandri Bridge was built from local grey stone.

documents.title: Kthandri river
documents.text: The Kthandri Bridge spans the slow Andri river.

documents.title: Kthandri tolls
documents.text: The Kthandri Bridge once charged a small crossing toll.

documents.title: Kthandri lore
documents.text: Local lore claims the Kthandri Bridge is watched by a heron.

question: What is the boiling point of the alloy vorellium, in degrees Celsius?

documents.title: Vorellium boiling point
documents.text: Vorellium has a boiling point of 2470 degrees Celsius.

documents.title: Vorellium uses
documents.text: Vorellium is used in heat shields and crucibles.

documents.title: Vorellium discovery
documents.text: Vorellium was first synthesised in a metallurgy lab.

documents.title: Vorellium cost
documents.text: Vorellium is expensive due to energy-intensive synthesis.

documents.title: Vorellium density
documents.text: Vorellium is notably dense for its class of alloys.

documents.title: Vorellium appearance
documents.text: Vorellium has a faint bluish sheen when polished.

documents.title: Vorellium alloys
documents.text: Vorellium is sometimes blended with lighter metals.

documents.title: Vorellium handling
documents.text: Vorellium dust should be handled with care in labs.

question: Who is the author credited with the novel 'The Saltering Tide'?

documents.title: The Saltering Tide author
documents.text: The Saltering Tide is credited to the author Enna Korvil.

documents.title: Saltering Tide plot
documents.text: The Saltering Tide follows a fishing town through a drought.

documents.title: Saltering Tide reception
documents.text: The Saltering Tide was praised for its patient prose.

documents.title: Saltering Tide sequel
documents.text: A sequel to The Saltering Tide was announced but unpublished.

documents.title: Saltering Tide setting
documents.text: The Saltering Tide is set along a windswept coast.

documents.title: Saltering Tide adaptation
documents.text: A stage adaptation of The Saltering Tide toured briefly.

documents.title: Saltering Tide style
documents.text: The Saltering Tide is written in short, spare chapters.

documents.title: Saltering Tide awards
documents.text: The Saltering Tide was shortlisted for a regional prize.
